task: I’m thinking about doing a simple public-transit outing from downtown Chicago to Aurora, Illinois soon, and I want a practical snapshot I can actually look at in the browser. On Google, please search for the recommended public-transit route from Chicago to Aurora, Illinois and open the actual transit directions so you can tell me the main mode I’d be taking and the estimated total travel time; leave that directions page open so I can review the route myself. Since I may need a little context for rail connections and fare structure, then go to Metra’s BNSF line page and summarize what the BNSF line is, where it runs, and where on that page or site fare information is listed; if there’s a separate fares link, open that in another tab so I have a visual reference. After that, head to Weather.com and pull up the local 10-day forecast for Aurora, Illinois, then give me the high and low temperatures for the next 3 days so I can judge whether the trip will feel comfortable. Please send everything back as one concise trip-planning summary, and keep the Google transit directions tab and the relevant Metra page open for comparison.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully searched for "public transit directions downtown Chicago to Aurora Illinois" on Google, navigated to the Google Maps transit results page, and identified the main mode of transit (Metra BNSF train) and the estimated travel time (1 hr 39 min to 1 hr 47 min). The Google Maps directions page remained open throughout the process as requested.

R2: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Metra BNSF line page (https://metra.com/train-lines/bnsf). In its final summary, it correctly identified the line as the "BNSF" and described its route coverage, noting that it runs from "Chicago Union Station" west through various suburbs (Berwyn, Brookfield, LaGrange, Hinsdale, Downers Grove, Lisle, Naperville, Route 59) and ends at "Aurora." The agent also confirmed that the Metra BNSF page was kept open for the user.

R3: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Metra BNSF line page, identified the "Tickets" menu, and navigated to the "Fares" page (https://metra.com/fares) in a separate tab. The agent also used the fare calculator on the site to determine the specific fare for the trip. This satisfies the requirement to identify where fare information is listed and provide a relevant fares link/section.

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Weather.com 10-day forecast page for Aurora, Illinois (as confirmed by the URL in step 20). The agent then provided a summary in step 23 that included high and low temperatures for the next 3 days (Wed 27: 90°/57°, Thu 28: 79°/51°, Fri 29: 85°/58°). While the agent did not explicitly show the screenshot of the weather data in the final step, the navigation to the correct page was verified in the action history, and the data provided is consistent with a 10-day forecast.

R5: success - The agent successfully gathered all the required information: Google transit recommendations (mode and travel time), Metra BNSF context (line description and fare info location), and the 3-day weather forecast for Aurora. It integrated these findings into a single, concise summary in the final response. The agent also maintained the requested tabs (Google Maps and Metra pages) as confirmed by the action history.

The Google Maps directions page remained open throughout the process as requested.

In its final summary, it correctly identified the line as the "BNSF" and described its route coverage, noting that it runs from "Chicago Union Station" west through various suburbs (Berwyn, Brookfield, LaGrange, Hinsdale, Downers Grove, Lisle, Naperville, Route 59) and ends at "Aurora." The agent also confirmed that the Metra BNSF page was kept open for the user.

rubric_results.requirement: Identify where fare information for riding the BNSF line is listed, including any relevant fares link or section.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm the response points to a visible fare-information location on the BNSF line page or an opened fares tab/page on metra.com.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully navigated to the Metra BNSF line page, identified the "Tickets" menu, and navigated to the "Fares" page (https://metra.com/fares) in a separate tab. The agent also used the fare calculator on the site to determine the specific fare for the trip. This satisfies the requirement to identify where fare information is listed and provide a relevant fares link/section.

rubric_results.requirement: Report the forecasted high and low temperatures for the next 3 days from Weather.com’s 10-day forecast for Aurora, Illinois.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm a Weather.com 10-day forecast page for Aurora is open and the response includes three day-by-day high/low pairs matching the visible forecast.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully navigated to the Weather.com 10-day forecast page for Aurora, Illinois (as confirmed by the URL in step 20). The agent then provided a summary in step 23 that included high and low temperatures for the next 3 days (Wed 27: 90°/57°, Thu 28: 79°/51°, Fri 29: 85°/58°). While the agent did not explicitly show the screenshot of the weather data in the final step, the navigation to the correct page was verified in the action history, and the data provided is consistent with a 10-day forecast.

rubric_results.requirement: Return all findings as one concise trip-planning summary that combines the Google transit recommendation, BNSF line context, fare-info location, and 3-day weather outlook.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm the final response is a single concise integrated summary containing all required elements from steps 1 through 3.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully gathered all the required information: Google transit recommendations (mode and travel time), Metra BNSF context (line description and fare info location), and the 3-day weather forecast for Aurora. It integrated these findings into a single, concise summary in the final response. The agent also maintained the requested tabs (Google Maps and Metra pages) as confirmed by the action history.
